goal: Allow the AI bot and ordinary UI clicks to open module-owned objects in a shared right drawer through provider registration, without hard-coding module UI logic in the AI core.
global_constraints: Do not introduce a universal event bus in this MVP.
Only mode=view is in MVP scope.
Do not accept arbitrary URLs from the AI model or browser payload.
Build htmx_url only server-side through a registered provider.
Keep source adapters separate from UI panel providers.
Providers may reuse domain selectors and policies but must not move domain access logic into apps.core.
Unknown provider, object type, object id or mode must fail closed.
Opening a panel must update the page context selection after the HTMX load.
The tool ui.open_right_panel changes UI state only and does not require confirmation.
Write operations remain separate tools with the existing confirmation flow.
Update docs and PROJECT_STRUCTURE.yaml after structural changes.
cross_cutting_acceptance: The global right drawer host is available on the AI chat page and ordinary module pages.
Existing workorder card clicks still open the workorder detail panel.
Existing waiting-list row clicks still open the entry detail panel.
The AI can open a workorder through ui.open_right_panel.
The AI can open a waiting-list entry through ui.open_right_panel.
A denied or unknown object is not opened and returns a safe error.
The opened object becomes the current PageContextEnvelope selection.
ui.get_current_context after opening returns the selected object summary.
verification_commands: python manage.py test apps.core.tests apps.ai.tests apps.workorders.tests apps.waiting_list.tests
implementation_notes: Prefer apps/core/right_panels.py for the provider protocol and registry.
Use module AppConfig.ready() for provider registration, following the source adapter registration pattern.
For the first slice, return UI commands from the tool result and teach the chat browser clients to execute them.
The global drawer host should not remove existing module panel semantics until e2e verifies the migration.
deferred_decisions: Whether edit/create modes should later be supported through the same provider interface.
Whether UI commands should later be persisted as events in a dedicated event platform.
Whether providers should expose human-readable search/open aliases beyond source_code/object_type/object_id.
